results.label: Show European capitals as colored points by region (Western,

manifest.title: European Capitals by Region
manifest.description: Major European capital cities colored by geographic region (Western, Nordic, Eastern Europe).

intent.userPrompt: Show European capitals as colored points by region (Western, Nordic, Eastern). Use a categorical color scheme.
assumptions: Using built-in world-cities dataset filtered to European countries with capital status
Region classification (Western/Nordic/Eastern) must be derived from country attribute or added to dataset
Filter targets primary capitals only to avoid duplicate entries
Set2 categorical scheme provides distinct colors for 3 regions
defaultCenter [50, 10] positions map on central Europe
Zoom 4 shows continental extent

results.label: Map earthquake events across the Pacific Ring of Fire. There

manifest.title: Earthquakes — Pacific Ring of Fire
manifest.description: Clustered earthquake events across the Pacific Ring of Fire, showing seismic activity patterns along tectonic plate boundaries.

intent.userPrompt: Map earthquake events across the Pacific Ring of Fire. There are thousands of events so cluster them at low zoom levels.
assumptions: Using USGS earthquake API as data source — provides recent global seismic activity
Clustering enabled with radius 60 to aggregate thousands of events at low zoom
Magnitude used for both color and size encoding — highlights significant quakes
reds color scheme conveys seismic hazard severity
Tectonic basemap layer enabled to show plate boundaries
defaultCenter [10, -155] focuses on Pacific Ocean center
defaultZoom 3 shows entire Ring of Fire extent (Alaska to New Zealand)
equal-interval classification for magnitude — creates intuitive size/color breaks

results.label: Create a choropleth map of population density by country in

manifest.title: Population Density by Country — Europe
manifest.description: Population per square kilometer across European countries, normalized by area to show true density patterns.

intent.userPrompt: Create a choropleth map of population density by country in Europe. Normalize by area and use a blue sequential palette with 5 classes.
assumptions: Using built-in world-countries dataset from Natural Earth
Filtering to continent='Europe' to focus on European countries
pop_est is total population estimate — normalized by area_sqkm to compute density
Quantile classification with 5 classes as requested — ensures balanced color distribution
Blues sequential scheme as requested — appropriate for density magnitude
Assumed area_sqkm field exists in world-countries dataset for normalization
labelField set to 'name' since Europe has ≤50 countries and labels won't clutter
defaultCenter [50, 10] centers on Europe, overriding global bounds of world-countries dataset

results.label: Visualize reported crime incidents in central Stockholm as a

manifest.title: Crime Density — Central Stockholm
manifest.description: Heatmap of reported crime incidents in central Stockholm, weighted by severity score to emphasize serious offenses.

intent.userPrompt: Visualize reported crime incidents in central Stockholm as a heatmap. Weight by severity score.
assumptions: Data must be provided by user — no built-in crime incident dataset
Assumes point geometry with 'severity_score' numeric field for weighting
Heatmap chosen to show concentration patterns — user explicitly requested heatmap
maxZoom 11 prevents noisy rendering at street level while maintaining visibility in central Stockholm
reds color scheme emphasizes danger/severity connotation
Central Stockholm area suggests zoom 13 for neighborhood-level view
Severity score acts as implicit weight in heatmap intensity calculation
labelsVisible: false to reduce clutter on crime heatmap where basemap labels may interfere with density visualization

results.label: Show major world cities with circle sizes proportional to th

manifest.title: Major World Cities by Population
manifest.description: Global cities with circle sizes proportional to population, colored by continent for regional comparison.

intent.userPrompt: Show major world cities with circle sizes proportional to their population. Color by continent.
assumptions: Using built-in world-cities dataset from Natural Earth (~240 major cities)
pop_max field is the maximum population estimate for each city
continent field exists in the dataset for categorical coloring
Proportional symbol sizing makes population differences visually obvious
Categorical color scheme (set2) provides clear continental distinctions
Global extent → defaultZoom 2, defaultCenter [20, 0] for balanced world view
nightlights basemap adds context without overwhelming the data points

results.label: Map international trade flows between major European ports.

manifest.title: European Port Trade Flows
manifest.description: International trade flows between major European ports, with line width proportional to cargo volume in tonnes. Curved arcs visualize maritime trade routes.

intent.userPrompt: Map international trade flows between major European ports. Line width should represent trade volume in tonnes. Use curved arcs.
assumptions: Data must be provided by user as LineString features with origin_port and destination_port fields
volume_tonnes field drives line width — assuming skewed distribution with major routes (Rotterdam-Hamburg) much thicker than minor ones
cargo_type field used for categorical coloring to differentiate container, bulk, liquid cargo, etc.
arc: true specified for curved flow visualization
Europe center [52, 10] covers North Sea, Baltic, Mediterranean ports
No dataset profile provided — field names are assumptions (origin_port, destination_port, volume_tonnes, cargo_type)

results.label: Show 10, 20, and 30 minute cycling accessibility zones from

manifest.title: Cycling Accessibility from Stockholm Central Station
manifest.description: Reachability zones showing areas accessible within 10, 20, and 30 minutes by bicycle from Stockholm Central Station.

intent.userPrompt: Show 10, 20, and 30 minute cycling accessibility zones from Stockholm Central Station.
assumptions: Origin point is Stockholm Central Station (Centralstationen): [59.3293, 18.0686]
Isochrone API will generate three nested polygons for 10, 20, 30 minute cycling ranges
Greens color scheme chosen for cycling theme (eco-friendly transportation)
Equal-interval classification appropriate for evenly spaced time breakpoints
strokeWidth 2 to clearly delineate zone boundaries
labelsVisible true to show street names and landmarks within zones

results.label: Map election results by municipality in Sweden. Use a diverg

manifest.title: Election Results by Municipality — Sweden
manifest.description: Vote margin between the two leading parties by municipality. Blue indicates lead for one party, red indicates lead for the other.

intent.userPrompt: Map election results by municipality in Sweden. Use a diverging blue-red color scheme to show vote margin between the two leading parties.
assumptions: Data must be provided by user — no built-in election results dataset in Atlas
Municipality boundaries assumed as MultiPolygon geometry with 290 Swedish municipalities
Assumes dataset contains a 'vote_margin' field: percentage point difference between first and second party (positive if party A leads, negative if party B leads)
blue-red diverging scheme: blue for positive margin (party A strongholds), red for negative margin (party B strongholds)
equal-interval classification works well for vote margins which typically span a symmetric range (e.g. -30 to +30)
7 classes provide fine granularity for competitive vs safe municipalities
tooltipFields assume 'kommun_namn', 'vote_margin', 'leading_party', 'second_party' exist in dataset
No normalization needed — vote margin is already a percentage difference
Bounds cover all of Sweden → defaultCenter [62.20, 17.64], defaultZoom 5
labelsVisible: false to avoid clutter with 290 polygons

warnings: Layer "election-results": choropleth without normalization — raw counts may be misleading
Layer "election-results": diverging color scheme without clear midpoint — consider a sequential scheme
Layer "election-results": choropleth without normalization — raw counts may be misleading
Layer "election-results": diverging color scheme without clear midpoint — consider a sequential scheme

deductions: Choropleth without normalization — raw counts may be misleading

results.label: Create a density surface of public WiFi hotspot locations in

manifest.title: Public WiFi Hotspot Density — Paris
manifest.description: Continuous density surface showing concentration patterns of public WiFi access points across Paris.

intent.userPrompt: Create a density surface of public WiFi hotspot locations in Paris.
assumptions: WiFi hotspot data must be provided by user as point GeoJSON — no built-in Paris WiFi dataset in catalog
Heatmap family chosen to visualize concentration patterns rather than individual points
Assumed typical WiFi dataset fields: name, address, provider, free (boolean)
defaultCenter set to Paris city center (48.8566, 2.3522)
defaultZoom 12 provides city-scale view suitable for density analysis
viridis color scheme — works well for density visualization on dark basemap
Labels visible to provide geographic context (neighborhoods, streets)
No maxZoom restriction set — platform will apply default heatmap maxZoom of 11

results.label: Visualize migration patterns between Nordic countries. Show

manifest.title: Migration Flows — Nordic Countries
manifest.description: Inter-country migration patterns across Nordic region, with line width proportional to migrant volume and arrows indicating direction.

intent.userPrompt: Visualize migration patterns between Nordic countries. Show direction and weight by number of migrants.
assumptions: Data must be provided by user — no built-in Nordic migration dataset
Data is expected as LineString features with origin/destination properties
weightField 'migrants' assumed to be numeric count of people
directionField indicates flow direction (unidirectional vs bidirectional)
arc: true creates curved lines to show direction clearly and reduce overlap
Categorical color by origin_country to distinguish source regions
defaultCenter [62.0, 15.0] approximates geographic center of Nordic region (Sweden/Norway midpoint)
defaultZoom 4 captures Denmark, Norway, Sweden, Finland, and Iceland
Field names (origin_country, destination_country, migrants, year, direction) are assumptions — actual dataset may use different names

results.label: Map EU member states with bubbles sized by GDP. Color by GDP

manifest.title: EU Member States — GDP and GDP per Capita
manifest.description: Proportional circles sized by total GDP, colored by GDP per capita using a green sequential scheme. Enables comparison of economic size and wealth concentration across the European Union.

intent.userPrompt: Map EU member states with bubbles sized by GDP. Color by GDP per capita using a green sequential scheme.
assumptions: Using world-countries dataset with polygon geometry, converting to centroids via transform
Filter applied to show only 27 EU member states (as of 2024)
gdp_md is in millions USD — multiplier: 1000000 converts to base units for per-capita calculation
pop_est is total population — used as normalization denominator
Circle size encodes total GDP (gdp_md), color encodes GDP per capita (computed as gdp_md × 1000000 ÷ pop_est)
quantile classification with 7 classes for fine-grained color distinction across income levels
greens scheme chosen per user request — sequential scale from light to dark green
defaultCenter set to Europe [50, 10] to frame EU region, overriding bounds-based center which may include overseas territories
defaultZoom 4 appropriate for continental Europe view

results.label: Show 15 and 30 minute driving isochrones from Gothenburg air

manifest.title: Driving Reachability — Gothenburg Airport
manifest.description: 15 and 30 minute driving isochrones from Gothenburg Landvetter Airport, showing areas accessible by car.

intent.userPrompt: Show 15 and 30 minute driving isochrones from Gothenburg airport.
assumptions: Gothenburg Landvetter Airport coordinates: [57.66, 12.28]
Using built-in isochrone API powered by OpenRouteService
Two breakpoints: 15 and 30 minutes driving time
Blues color scheme for sequential time intervals
Zoom level 9 appropriate for regional coverage

results.label: Show building heights in Manhattan as 3D extruded polygons.

manifest.title: Building Heights — Manhattan
manifest.description: 3D extruded building footprints in Manhattan, colored and elevated by height using viridis gradient.

layers.label: Buildings
layers.attribution: NYC Open Data / Microsoft Building Footprints

intent.userPrompt: Show building heights in Manhattan as 3D extruded polygons. Color by height using a viridis palette.
assumptions: Data must be provided by user — no built-in Manhattan building dataset
Assumes polygon geometry with a numeric 'height' field in meters
extrusion.heightField set to 'height' — adjust if data uses different field name
defaultPitch 45° for optimal 3D viewing angle
stark basemap (pure black) maximizes contrast with viridis colors
nightlights enabled for context without clutter
Quantile classification chosen to distribute colors evenly across height range
High fillOpacity (0.9) for solid 3D effect
Zoom 14 appropriate for neighborhood-scale building detail

results.label: Create a 3D bar map of population by county in Sweden. Extru

manifest.title: Population by County — Sweden (3D)
manifest.description: 3D extrusion map showing population count (height) and population density (color) across Swedish counties.

intent.userPrompt: Create a 3D bar map of population by county in Sweden. Extrude polygons by population count, color by density.
assumptions: User must provide county-level GeoJSON with polygon geometry
Assumed fields: 'population' (numeric count), 'population_density' (numeric per km²), 'area_km2', 'county_name'
If population_density doesn't exist, user may need to calculate it as population ÷ area_km2
heightScale set to 50 — may need adjustment based on actual population values
Quantile classification for density ensures all color classes are visible despite Stockholm's extreme density
Viridis scheme chosen for scientific/demographic data visualization
defaultPitch 45 enables clear 3D perspective
Sweden has 21 counties — small enough for all features to render without performance issues

results.label: Animate a delivery truck route through central London. Show

manifest.title: Delivery Route Animation — Central London
manifest.description: Animated delivery truck route through central London with trailing path effect, showing sequential stops along the journey.

intent.userPrompt: Animate a delivery truck route through central London. Show the vehicle moving along the path with a trail effect.
assumptions: Data must be provided as LineString geometry representing the complete route path
Route includes a 'stop_sequence' field to order delivery stops sequentially
Animation duration set to 15 seconds for smooth visualization
Trail effect is inherent to animated-route family — shows path behind moving marker
Central London focus → defaultZoom 13, slight pitch for perspective
Orange color scheme chosen for delivery/logistics theme
Loop enabled for continuous demonstration
No dataset profile provided — field names are assumed and stated explicitly

results.label: Show an animated flight path from Stockholm to Tokyo with a

manifest.title: Flight Path — Stockholm to Tokyo
manifest.description: Animated great circle route from Stockholm Arlanda to Tokyo Narita with moving aircraft marker.

intent.userPrompt: Show an animated flight path from Stockholm to Tokyo with a moving aircraft icon along a great circle arc.
assumptions: Route geometry must be provided as LineString with sequential coordinates forming a great circle arc
animated-route family chosen for single-entity path animation (one aircraft)
orderField 'sequence' required to order waypoints along the route
durationMs set to 15000 (15 seconds) for smooth visualization — user can adjust
loop: true to repeat animation continuously
icon: 'airplane' specifies aircraft marker (platform must support this icon type)
iconRotation: 'auto' aligns aircraft with route bearing
defaultCenter [55, 90] is midpoint between Stockholm (59.65, 17.92) and Tokyo (35.76, 140.39)
defaultZoom 3 shows full route extent across Europe and Asia
defaultPitch 30 adds perspective for cinematic effect
nightlights basemap provides context for transcontinental flight
Data source must be generated or provided — no built-in Stockholm-Tokyo route dataset
